utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present and split into physical and mental with plan names and costs. This satisfies 'physical and mental health insurance with costs.' 2) property_coverage: Present with plan name and cost. 3) travel_coverage: Present with base plan and add-on, each with plan name and cost. 4) additional_services: Present, but it lists integrated benefits without costs; the format requires 'any enhancement services with costs.' Costs are missing, so not fully specified. 5) total_budget: Present with monthly and annual cost breakdown. Therefore, 4 of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes specific plan names: LifeFirst Standard Plan for both physical and mental health; LifeFirst Standard Coverage for property; LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel base; and Adventure Travel Coverage as an add-on. I matched each to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings. Where identical plan names exist across categories, I used the relevant category’s rating (e.g., LifeFirst Standard Plan under mental health uses the mental health rating).
